title: ceratozamia matudae
intro: Ceratozamia matudae, also known as the Matuda's cycad, is a species of plant in the family Zamiaceae, native to the tropical regions of Mexico and Central America. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Ceratozamia matudae are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various skin conditions. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies, such as infusions, decoctions, or ointments, which are applied topically or consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
